sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Looking at the current image, the character has moved down and left. It's now overlapping with the yellow "P" object. Let me check the current state to see what changed.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state after moving down and left
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("=== Objects ===")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['W', 'R', 'N', 'Y', 'b']:
        b = n['boundary']
        minr = min(r for r,c in b)
        maxr = max(r for r,c in b)
        minc = min(c for r,c in b)
        maxc = max(c for r,c in b)
        print(f"id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} bbox=({minr},{maxr},{minc},{maxc})")

# Check adjacency for white
print("\n=== Adjacency (W objects) ===")
for pair in seg['adjacency_list']:
    i, j = pair
    ci = next((n['color'] for n in seg['nodes'] if n['id'] == i), '?')
    cj = next((n['color'] for n in seg['nodes'] if n['id'] == j), '?')
    if ci == 'W' or cj == 'W':
        print(f"{i}({ci}) - {j}({cj})")

# Count red blocks
red_count = sum(1 for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'R')
print(f"\nRed block count: {red_count}")

# Check if yellow object changed
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'Y':
        print(f"Yellow pixels: {n['pixels']}")

# Check if green object changed
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        print(f"Green pixels: {n['pixels']}")

# Check if blue object changed
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'b':
        print(f"Blue pixels: {n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
